mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.5% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.16% as of 2023-02-15 indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 1.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment exceeding 200,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 200,000 persons in a single month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite positive payroll signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which compresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,155 thousand units as of 2023-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite dual demand contraction signal, or the interaction effect (joint decline) shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production below 104.0 (index 2017=100) signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, which constrains profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 104.0 and remains there for at least 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.739 as of 2023-02-15 is near threshold
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 104.0 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% while the federal funds rate remains above 4.0%, the Fed faces a policy dilemma between supporting employment and controlling inflation, creating uncertainty that depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% and FEDFUNDS at 1.16% as of 2023-02-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite policy dilemma signal, or the interaction term (UNRATE × FEDFUNDS) shows no incremental explanatory power beyond individual coefficients (R² improvement < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 1% signals strengthening consumer demand and discretionary spending, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases by more than 1.0% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 474,841 million dollars as of 2023-02-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite strong retail sales signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over any 3-month rolling window
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-stage recession
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following unemployment spikes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,100 thousand units while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 245.0, the combination signals both demand weakness and persistent cost pressures on homebuilders and construction firms, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1,100 thousand units AND CPIAUCSL > 245.0 simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,155 thousand and CPIAUCSL at 245.03 as of 2023-01-15
regime_hint: stagflation risk with housing market weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite housing weakness and inflation signal, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (R² improvement < 0.015).
